{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks whether GRG5 is involved only in late embryonic mouse development. The provided documents from MEDCORP mention that Lgr5+ stem cells (which are related to GRG5, as GRG5 is a leucine-rich repeat-containing G-protein coupled receptor 5) develop during the growth of intestinal villi and crypts and are essential for the biochemical balance of the intestine. These stem cells are also present in the formed intestine. This indicates that GRG5/Lgr5 is involved in intestinal stem cell development and function, which occurs not only in late embryonic stages but also in postnatal and adult life. The presence of these stem cells in the formed intestine suggests activity beyond late embryonic development. Therefore, GRG5 is not limited to late embryonic mouse development. The UMLS documents do not provide any relevant information about GRG5 or its developmental timeline. Based on the MEDCORP document, GRG5 is involved in postnatal and adult intestinal function, which contradicts the claim that it is involved only in late embryonic development.", "answer_choice": "B"}
